items.name: Minicor
items.source_story_title: Launch HN: Minicor (YC P26) – Windows desktop automations at scale
items.summary: Windows desktop automation at scale, built around Python RPA, replay logs, and MCP debugging.
items.why_included: Concrete launch thread with detailed operational discussion about legacy-app automation.
items.evidence: HN commenters discussed Python RPA, VM replay logs, deterministic workflows, and agent-assisted debugging.

items.name: DynIP
items.source_story_title: DynIP – Dynamic DNS with RFC 2136, IPv6, DNSSEC, and BYOD
items.summary: Dynamic DNS platform with RFC 2136, IPv6, DNSSEC, and bring-your-own-domain support.
items.why_included: Clear product discussion around modern DNS features and deployment workflows.
items.evidence: Commenters highlighted RFC 2136/TSIG updates, IPv6, DNSSEC, BYOD, and Docker-based updater tooling.

items.name: Rosalind
items.source_story_title: Rosalind: A genomics toolkit in Rust running whole-genome pipelines on a laptop
items.summary: Rust genomics toolkit for whole-genome pipelines on a laptop.
items.why_included: Concrete open-source toolkit with active discussion about Rust adoption in bioinformatics.
items.evidence: HN commenters were interested in Rust for computational biology and debated the project’s novelty and validation.

items.name: Geist Pixel
items.source_story_title: A few interesting modern pixel fonts
items.summary: Vercel’s pixel display font for expressive UI, logos, and system-style text.
items.why_included: Strong positive mention in a thread full of concrete font recommendations.
items.evidence: Commenters said Geist Pixel was “not a novelty font” but a “system extension,” and compared it to other pixel fonts.

items.name: Two Slice
items.source_story_title: A few interesting modern pixel fonts
items.summary: A 2-pixel-high bitmap font that still manages to be readable.
items.why_included: Direct reader praise for readability despite extreme size constraints.
items.evidence: A commenter called Two Slice “shockingly readable” and singled it out as surprisingly legible.

items.name: Departure Mono
items.source_story_title: A few interesting modern pixel fonts
items.summary: Monospaced pixel font with a lo-fi technical vibe.
items.why_included: Named by commenters as a favorite among modern pixel fonts.
items.evidence: A commenter recommended Departure Mono as another favorite modern pixel font.

items.name: Topaz
items.source_story_title: A few interesting modern pixel fonts
items.summary: Classic bitmap font still beloved for terminals and IDEs.
items.why_included: Commenters still use it and explicitly called it a favorite.
items.evidence: One commenter said they wanted a better Topaz and called it their favorite font.

items.name: Vellum
items.source_story_title: I Bypassed Adobe and Microsoft to Build a Git-Tracked Book Production Pipeline
items.summary: Mac ebook-formatting software for polished EPUB and print output.
items.why_included: A commenter recommended it as the better option for fiction EPUB workflows.
items.evidence: HN discussion contrasted Vellum with clunky InDesign EPUB output and recommended it for fiction publishing.

items.name: Standard Ebooks
items.source_story_title: I Bypassed Adobe and Microsoft to Build a Git-Tracked Book Production Pipeline
items.summary: Free, standards-driven ebook project with careful typography and formatting.
items.why_included: Suggested as a high-quality baseline for accessible ebook production.
items.evidence: A commenter recommended Standard Ebooks as a solid baseline for standards-compliant, accessible ebooks.
